content: For promised movement animation, capture before/mid/after frames and verify the object has a real intermediate frame instead of teleporting from source to destination.
For capture/hit/explosion effects, verify the effect appears during the short event window and disappears afterward.
Verified animation sessions should audit before/mid/after frames so smooth motion is not implemented as teleporting.
When an action changes (idle->walk, walk->attack), reset the animation frame index to 0 so the new clip plays from its first frame instead of inheriting a stale frame and showing a single held pose.
Draw art that faces RIGHT by default and mirror for left movement with a ctx.save()/ctx.scale(-1,1)/ctx.restore() wrapper; do not generate separate left/right sprites or the facings drift visually.
A walk/run cycle must advance its frame index over time while moving (timer-driven, e.g. every ~120ms), not hold one frame; verify the drawn frame changes across a ~500ms movement window.

On death or destruction, play a brief fade/shrink/particle burst over a few frames before removing the entity, instead of the sprite vanishing in a single frame; verify intermediate frames exist during the removal window.

When physics runs on a fixed timestep, interpolate render position between the previous and current state by the accumulator fraction so motion is smooth at high refresh rates rather than stepping; verify motion is not visibly quantized.
On taking damage, flash or tint the sprite for a short window then return to its normal appearance; verify the flash is transient and does not leave the entity permanently tinted.
Keep a subtle idle animation (breathing, bob, blink) playing when the character is not moving, instead of freezing on one static frame; verify the drawn frame still changes over a window while idle.

Chess/holochess: a legal move must show intermediate motion — walk frames cycling while draw coords tween square-by-square; board[] commits only after the path animation completes. Teleporting (instant board update with no mid-move frames) fails this audit.
